free_text: Extension of the energy range of the experimental
products of proton induced nuclear reactions on
natural Dy foils as target, natural Ti foils as
monitor foils and Al foils as energy degraders were
irradiated with proton beam of 65 MeV, 25 nA for 1 hr.
Gamma spectra were measured nondestructively in
4 series with cooling time intervals: 22-31.6 hr,
The beam energy uncertainty at the middle of
each foil was estimated by combining uncertainties
from energy degradation (due to foil thickness) and
energy straggling. The initial beam energy uncertainty
was +/-0.3 MeV and 0.8 MeV for the last Dy foil in the
The cross-section uncertainty was estimated as
the square root of the sum in quadrature of all
Fitted excitation functions were used for
Includes decay from higher energy isomer states.
